content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN, initially: A1=A2=⋯=AN=0.A_1 = A_2 = \cdots = A_N = 0.A1​=A2​=⋯=AN​=0.

Chef wants to transform this array into a permutation of the integers 1,2,…,N{1, 2, \dots, N}1,2,…,N.

Chef may perform the following operation any number of times:


Select an index iii such that 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.
Update the value: Ai:=Ai+1A_i := A_i + 1Ai​:=Ai​+1.
After every operation, compute ZZZ as follows:


ZZZ is the number of indices jjj such that 1≤j<N1 \le j < N1≤j<N,
and both adjacent positions are zero: Aj=0A_j = 0Aj​=0 and Aj+1=0A_{j+1} = 0Aj+1​=0.
Chef’s score is the sum of ZZZ over all operations performed.

Your task is to determine the minimum possible score Chef can achieve while converting the array into a permutation of 1,2,…,N{1, 2, \dots, N}1,2,…,N.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line:

The line contains one integer NNN — the length of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer, the minimum score that can be obtained.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the 1st test case, initially a = [0]. After first operation, a = [1]. So Z=0.
In the 3rd test case, initially a = [0, 0, 0, 0].

Apply first operation on index 2, so a = [0, 1, 0, 0]. So Z = 1.
Apply second operation on index 3, a = [0, 1, 1, 0]. For this operation, Z = 0.
For all subsequent operations, Z will be 0. So summation of Z across all the operations will be 1.
